step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** The provided evidence consistently identifies B-cell lymphomas—specifically mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma, follicular lymphoma, and diffuse large B-cell lymphoma—as the most common types of non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma affecting the orbit. These are all subsets of mature B-cell neoplasms. No evidence supports T-cell, NK-cell, or plasma cell lymphoma being more prevalent in orbital pathology. Rituximab use targeting CD20-positive B-cells further confirms the predominance of B-cell lineage in orbital lymphomas.
